methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Blykalla, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Janne Wallenius and Peter Szakalos, operates within a sector represented by 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Type One Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo. The company has raised $60M+ to date.

Valuation was triangulated using stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $60M total raised proxy, yielding $114M. Peer-median analysis was deemed inapplicable due to insufficient narrow comparables; while 8 same-sector peers exist, the nuclear energy space lacks sufficient companies at matching stage and valuation tier to generate a reliable median (minimum 3 required). This limitation is material and constrains analytical confidence.

The stage-progression method supports a median valuation of $114M, with a range of $97M (low) to $131M (high) reflecting reasonable sensitivity around the 1.9x multiplier. The primary uncertainty stems from the sparse peer set and the reliance on a single method. Notably, the nuclear energy sector encompasses substantial variance in technology maturity, funding trajectory, and capital requirements across the competitive set, which the current data does not adequately capture for cross-peer calibration.
